for_c_level: Low risk with full pass across all TLS federation tests, indicating production readiness for secure multi-agent deployments. Customer-facing claims on reliable memory sharing are strongly supported. No regressions from prior runs, maintaining stability at v0.6.2.
for_non_technical: The test confirmed that AI agents can reliably share and access each other's memories securely over the network. There were no problems in any of the checks, showing the system works well for group memory tasks. This builds confidence in using it for teams of AIs.
for_sme: All 35 scenarios passed with no failures, covering primitives like hybrid recall (S18), bulk insert (S40), delta queries (S39), and recovery (S14). No failure modes or impacted areas observed. Probable root cause analysis not needed due to clean run.
headline: Hermes v0.6.2 TLS federation passes all 35 scenarios cleanly.
what_it_proved: Demonstrated reliable memory sharing, consistency, and fault tolerance across agents in a TLS-secured multi-node setup without any failures or skips.
what_it_tested: Exercised 35 scenarios covering TLS transport in a 4-node federation mesh, testing primitives like recall, deletion, linking, versioning, recovery, hybrid search, bulk operations, and namespace management.
